question: Based FDV above $2B one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Based's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Based (https://x.com/BasedOneX) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Kim Kardashian pass the bar exam by May 3?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kim Kardashian receives a passing score on the California Bar Exam by May 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the results of all California State Bar Exams within this market's timeframe are not known by the specified end date, this market may remain open until May 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, after which, if results remain unavailable, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the California State Bar Association, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will Kim Kardashian pass the bar exam by May 3?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kim Kardashian receives a passing score on the California Bar Exam by May 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the results of all California State Bar Exams within this market's timeframe are not known by the specified end date, this market may remain open until May 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, after which, if results remain unavailable, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the California State Bar Association, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 82.5% implied probability for Kim Kardashian passing the California bar exam by May 3, driven primarily by the absence of any official confirmation that she sat for the February 2024 exam or appears on the upcoming results list, expected around early May from the State Bar of California. After passing the baby bar on her fourth try in 2021 via apprenticeship study, Kardashian has prioritized her SKIMS empire, Hulu reality series, and family amid rumors of stalled legal ambitions—no verified reports indicate she completed the required four years of supervised study or registered for the test. Public skepticism, fueled by her high-profile distractions and historical exam struggles, reinforces trader caution ahead of the resolution deadline, with historical bar pass rates hovering below 50% for first-timers adding to the odds.

question: Will Waymo launch in New York City by June 30 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Waymo begins offering its ride-hailing service to the general public within the listed city by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

Any taxi service available to the general public which operates without a human driver actively controlling the vehicle will count, regardless of membership or other financial restrictions.

A qualifying launch requires the general public be able to hail a Waymo vehicle within the listed city \ through the Waymo One app or another official Waymo platform (such as a dedicated website or integrated partner app like Uber). 

Limited pilot programs, internal employee testing, or invite-only service will not qualify.

The primary resolution source is official information from Waymo (see: https://waymo.com/rides/c), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Which cities will Waymo launch in by June 30?

The primary resolution source is official information from Waymo (see: https://waymo.com/rides/c), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Waymo's ongoing testing and employee ride-hailing in Austin, Texas—paired with regulatory approvals from local authorities—have propelled trader consensus toward high implied probabilities for a public launch there by June 30, 2025, potentially marking its fourth U.S. city after Phoenix, San Francisco, and Los Angeles. Official Alphabet statements confirm expansion plans for Austin and Atlanta in 2025, but historical AV delays (e.g., Cruise's setbacks) temper optimism, with speculation swirling around Miami or Washington, D.C. Competitive pressures from Tesla's upcoming robotaxi event and Zoox's testing intensify scrutiny, while Alphabet's Q3 earnings in late October could provide timeline updates critical for resolution criteria.

question: Will Waymo operate in 5 cities or less on June 30 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of distinct cities in which Waymo’s ride-hailing service is publicly available, either through the Waymo One app or a partner platform such as Uber, as of June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET.

A city counts if riders can book a Waymo vehicle through either the Waymo One app or the Uber app at that time. Any taxi service provided by Waymo that is available to the general public and operates without a human driver actively controlling the vehicle will count, regardless of membership or other financial restrictions.

Limited pilot programs, internal employee testing, or invite-only service will not qualify.

If Waymo describes a broader region (e.g., “Los Angeles County” or “San Francisco Bay Area”) as a single service area, it will count as one city/region for this market.

The primary resolution source is official information from Waymo (see: https://waymo.com/rides/), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: How many cities will Waymo operate in by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of distinct cities in which Waymo’s ride-hailing service is publicly available, either through the Waymo One app or a partner platform such as Uber, as of June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET.

A city counts if riders can book a Waymo vehicle through either the Waymo One app or the Uber app at that time. Any taxi service available to the general public which operates without a human driver actively controlling the vehicle will count, regardless of membership or other financial restrictions.Limited pilot programs, internal employee testing, or invite-only service will not qualify.

If Waymo describes a broader region (e.g., “Los Angeles County” or “San Francisco Bay Area”) as a single service area, it will count as one city/region for this market.

The primary resolution source is official information from Waymo (see: https://waymo.com/rides/c), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors aggressive expansion, with a 36.5% implied probability for Waymo operating in 12+ cities by June 30, driven by its recent commercial robotaxi launch in Austin via Uber partnership in March 2024, bringing confirmed public service to four metro areas: Phoenix, San Francisco, Los Angeles, and Austin. Supporting this sentiment are Alphabet's Q1 earnings touting over 100,000 weekly paid rides and a $5 billion funding commitment to scale the Jaguar I-PACE fleet, alongside May announcements for testing in Atlanta and plans for Washington, D.C., and Miami amid easing California regulations. However, new-city rollouts hinge on mapping completion and permits, tempering odds for 11 (22%) and 10 (17.5%) while downplaying ≤5 at just 6.3%, as historical delays caution against over-optimism.

description:
This market will resolve according to the number of distinct cities in which Waymo’s ride-hailing service is publicly available, either through the Waymo One app or a partner platform such as Uber, as of June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET.

A city counts if riders can book a Waymo vehicle through either the Waymo One app or the Uber app at that time. Any taxi service provided by Waymo that is available to the general public and operates without a human driver actively controlling the vehicle will count, regardless of membership or other financial restrictions.

Limited pilot programs, internal employee testing, or invite-only service will not qualify.

If Waymo describes a broader region (e.g., “Los Angeles County” or “San Francisco Bay Area”) as a single service area, it will count as one city/region for this market.

The primary resolution source is official information from Waymo (see: https://waymo.com/rides/), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Washington Commanders agree to name stadium after Trump?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Washington Commanders, or the ownership/development entity responsible for the team’s planned new stadium in Washington, D.C., officially announce and agree that the stadium will be named after Donald Trump, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

A qualifying announcement must:
- Be made publicly and on the record by the Commanders organization or by the entity that owns or controls the new stadium (e.g., via press release, official statement, or filing).
- Clearly state that the new stadium will bear Trump’s name (for example, “Trump Stadium” or “Donald J. Trump Field”).
- Reflect a finalized agreement or naming-rights deal, not a proposal, negotiation, or expression of intent.

If any other official naming-rights deal is announced for the new stadium prior to June 30, 2026 — including a corporate sponsorship or another individual’s name — this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution sources will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: No official announcement from Washington Commanders ownership or the NFL supports renaming FedExField after Donald Trump, anchoring the 93.8% "No" trader consensus as the stadium's naming rights contract with FedEx runs through 2027. Owner Josh Harris, focused on the team's rebuild amid a 4-5 start and quarterback uncertainty with Jayden Daniels nursing minor injuries, has prioritized franchise stability over political gestures that could alienate fans or sponsors in a league wary of partisanship. Post-election social media buzz remains unsubstantiated rumor, with historical NFL stadium deals—like SoFi or Allegiant—driven by corporate bids, not celebrity endorsements, underscoring low likelihood amid the Commanders' push for playoff relevance.

question: Will Trump create a tariff dividend by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Trump Administration formally creates (e.g., by signing federal legislation or performing executive action) a tariff dividend/rebate by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any bill signed into law or executive action taken within this market's time frame will qualify, regardless of when the law or action goes into effect.

A qualifying payment of any amount distributed to any segment of individual US taxpayers will qualify as long as it is clearly attributed primarily to tariff revenue rather than a routine tax refund or credit.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus at 98.6% for "No" stems from the extraordinarily tight timeline: President-elect Trump assumes office January 20, 2025, leaving just over 70 days to implement tariffs and generate a tangible "dividend"—presumed as revenue rebates or economic offsets from duties on imports like those proposed on China, Mexico, and Canada. No transition announcements detail such a mechanism, and tariffs demand regulatory rulemaking, customs enforcement, and revenue collection, processes historically spanning months. Recent developments prioritize cabinet confirmations over trade policy execution. Realistic shifts could arise from immediate executive orders or surprise fiscal maneuvers, though precedents like past administrations' trade actions highlight persistent delays.

question: Will Stephen Miller leave the Trump administration before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if it is announced that that the listed individual will leave the Trump Administration, or otherwise ceases to be a member of administration by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of the listed individual's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The Trump administration includes individuals formally appointed by Donald Trump to roles within the U.S. federal government, such as Cabinet members, Executive Office staff, senior policy advisors, ambassadors, or White House staff whose appointments are publicly announced by official government channels.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Trump administration, however a consensus of credible reopening may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: NVIDIA's explosive AI-driven revenue growth, with fiscal Q2 data center sales surging 154% year-over-year to $26.3 billion, has cemented its market cap lead at over $3.3 trillion—well ahead of Microsoft ($3.1T) and Apple ($3.2T)—fueling 98.8% implied probability on Polymarket that it remains the world's largest company by March 31 end. Trader consensus reflects unrelenting hyperscaler demand for H100/H200 GPUs and anticipation for Blackwell chip ramp-up in Q4, bolstering forward multiples despite a lofty 55x P/E. Realistic challenges include Q3 earnings misses on November 20, escalating U.S.-China export curbs eroding 20% of sales, or a tech sector rotation amid persistent inflation, potentially allowing Microsoft or Apple to reclaim the top spot.

events.title: Largest Company end of December 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the largest company in the world by market cap on December 31, 2026, as of market close.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: NVIDIA's commanding 66.5% implied probability as the largest company by market cap at end-December 2026 stems from its AI chip dominance, with data center revenue surging 94% year-over-year in Q3 2024 to fuel projections of 30-50% CAGR through 2026, outpacing peers amid hyperscaler capex boom. Currently at $3.42 trillion market cap—trailing Apple ($3.52T) by a slim margin—trader consensus reflects Blackwell GPU ramp-up and $5-10T valuation forecasts from analysts. Apple holds 14% on steady services growth and buybacks, despite China iPhone weakness; Alphabet's 13.5% ties to Google Cloud AI gains, while Tesla (2.5%) and Microsoft (2.1%) lag on execution risks versus NVIDIA's momentum, with Saudi Aramco (1.4%) vulnerable to oil volatility.

question: Discord IPO before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed company completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements or credible news sources. The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If the listed company merges with another entity, is acquired, or ceases to exist before the market resolves, the market will also resolve to "No".

This market will resolve early if the listed company completes an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for tech IPOs before 2027 tilts bullish at around 70% implied probability, propelled by 2024's IPO resurgence—over 40 listings including Reddit and Astera Labs amid Fed rate cuts and AI fervor boosting valuations. Major unicorns like Stripe ($70B valuation) and Databricks ($43B) have signaled 2025 targets despite CEO hesitations on market timing, while Chime and Klarna eye similar paths amid competitive secondary trading platforms like Forge Global offering liquidity alternatives. Regulatory tailwinds post-U.S. election and upcoming Q4 earnings could catalyze S-1 filings, though valuation resets remain a risk as historical precedents show 20-30% slips from private peaks. Watch Money20/20 conference for executive updates.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed company completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements or credible news sources. The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If the listed company merges with another entity, is acquired, or ceases to exist before the market resolves, the market will also resolve to "No".

This market will resolve early if the listed company completes an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for tech IPOs before 2027 tilts bullish at around 70% implied probability, propelled by 2024's IPO resurgence—over 40 listings including Reddit and Astera Labs amid Fed rate cuts and AI fervor boosting valuations. Major unicorns like Stripe ($70B valuation) and Databricks ($43B) have signaled 2025 targets despite CEO hesitations on market timing, while Chime and Klarna eye similar paths amid competitive secondary trading platforms like Forge Global offering liquidity alternatives. Regulatory tailwinds post-U.S. election and upcoming Q4 earnings could catalyze S-1 filings, though valuation resets remain a risk as historical precedents show 20-30% slips from private peaks. Watch Money20/20 conference for executive updates.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Elon Musk ceases to be CEO of Tesla for any length of time between November 11, 2025, and December 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement of Elon Musk's resignation/firing before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/firing goes into effect.

This market's primary resolution source will be official information from Tesla and/or Elon Musk, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tesla traders overwhelmingly back Elon Musk remaining CEO through 2027, with "No" at 90.5% implied probability, driven by June 2024 shareholder ratification of his massive compensation package after a Delaware court reversal, reaffirming board confidence amid Tesla's AI pivot. Musk's repeated emphasis on needing 25% voting control to lead autonomy efforts like Full Self-Driving and Optimus robots underscores his entrenched role, with no official signals of departure despite xAI distractions. Consensus holds as Tesla nears Q3 earnings on October 23 and robotaxi updates, though risks include voting impasse prompting a shift to xAI, regulatory blocks on autonomy, or sustained stock weakness forcing board intervention.

question: Will a Chinese AI model become #1 by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any model owned by a Chinese company has the highest arena score on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) by June 30, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If a Chinese AI model ties for #1 Arena score, it will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes."

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source becomes unavailable, the market will remain open until it is accessible again. If it becomes permanently unavailable, resolution will be based on another credible source.

U.S. export controls on advanced semiconductors, tightened by the Commerce Department in October 2024, continue limiting China's access to Nvidia H100 GPUs essential for frontier AI training, widening the compute gap. Recent releases like Qwen2.5 in January 2025 showed gains but failed to overtake leaders, reinforcing trader consensus that no Chinese model will claim #1 by June 30 amid ongoing U.S.-China tech restrictions.

question: Will OpenAI launch a new consumer hardware product by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI publicly announces and launches a new consumer hardware product by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

A "consumer hardware product" is defined as a physical device intended for direct use by individual consumers, rather than enterprise or developer tools. The product must be newly introduced and not a rebrand, update, or iteration of any previously released device.

Examples that would qualify include an AI-powered wearable, smart home assistant, or augmented reality device. Developer tools, AI chips, or servers marketed exclusively for enterprise use would not qualify.

The resolution source will be official information from OpenAI.

events.title: Will OpenAI launch a consumer hardware product by...?
events.description: This is a market on whether OpenAI will release a consumer hardware product by the specified date.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns low implied probabilities—under 20%—to an OpenAI consumer hardware launch by year-end, driven by the lack of official confirmation despite persistent rumors. Recent Financial Times reports clarified OpenAI's role as a key investor in Jony Ive's $6.5 billion-funded startup for screenless AI devices, with prototypes eyed for 2026 under a new brand, not OpenAI's direct product. This tempers hype, especially amid flops like Humane's AI Pin and Rabbit R1, highlighting hardware execution risks in AI wearables. Competitive pressure from Apple Intelligence and Google Gemini adds urgency, but watch Sam Altman's X posts or OpenAI's December developer updates for catalysts that could shift odds.

question: SpaceX Starship fully reusable before 2027?
description:
On February 28, Elon Musk posted that it was likely Starship would become fully reusable in 2025. You can see the X post here: https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1895598258225106984

This market will resolve to "Yes" if SpaceX or Elon Musk announces that Starship is fully reusable by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note, this market refers to the Starship upper stage and does not require reusability for the Super-Heavy booster.

For this market to resolve to "Yes" it is not necessary that an actual reuse occur, only that SpaceX or Elon Musk announces that Starship is fully reusable by this market's resolution date.

The resolution source for this market is announcements by Elon Musk and SpaceX, or their official representatives.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans 60% toward "No" on Starship achieving full reusability—defined as reliable booster and ship catch by tower arms—before 2027, driven by persistent heat shield erosion, Raptor engine refurbishment hurdles, and FAA regulatory delays following Flight 5's aborted booster catch in October 2024. Despite Super Heavy's controlled splashdowns and Ship's orbital reentry progress, tile losses and grid fin faults highlight the gap to rapid turnaround like Falcon 9. Elon Musk's optimistic 2025 targets contrast with historical timeline slips, tempering bets amid Flight 6 preparations expected late 2024 or early 2025, where a successful catch could shift odds sharply.

question: Will Tesla launch robotaxis in California by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Tesla publicly launches a self-driving taxi service in California by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any taxi service available to the general public which operates without a human driver actively controlling the vehicle will count, regardless of membership or other financial restrictions. 

Services which are limited to Tesla employees or a limited test group without general access will not qualify. 

This market's resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: OpenAI acquired before 2027?

question: Will GPT-6 be released by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI's GPT-6 model is made available to the general public by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

For this market to resolve to "Yes," GPT-6 must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be clearly defined and publicly announced by OpenAI as being accessible to the general public.

GPT-6 refers to a product explicitly named GPT-6 (e.g. ChatGPT-6o would count), or one that is recognized as a successor to GPT-5, similar to the progression from GPT-3 to GPT-4. Products labeled as GPT-5.5 or similar will not count for this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from OpenAI, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

question: AI wins IMO gold medal in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any AI gets a gold medal in the International Math Olympiad between January 1, 2026 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source is the IMO Grand Challenge (https://imo-grand-challenge.github.io/) and the Artificial Intelligence Math Olympiad (AIMO, https://aimoprize.com/). If either source demonstrates that an AI has won the challenge/prize before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes".

eventMetadata.context_description: Google DeepMind's AlphaProof and AlphaGeometry 2 systems securing silver medal performance at the 2024 International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO)—solving four of six problems for 28/42 points—anchor the 57.5% yes odds, signaling rapid advances in AI mathematical reasoning and formal proof generation. This leap from prior years' sub-bronze scores reflects scaling improvements in reinforcement learning and language model integration, with DeepMind explicitly targeting gold-level capabilities soon. Traders weigh the two-year runway to IMO 2026 against persistent hurdles like creative problem-solving on the toughest geometry and combinatorics questions, plus competition from OpenAI and others. Key catalysts include 2025 IMO results and forthcoming AI math benchmarks, tempering enthusiasm amid historical timeline slips in AI milestones.

question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Recent developments, including vetoes of state-level measures like California's AI safety bill and stalled federal proposals such as the AI Foundation Model Transparency Act, reinforce legislative gridlock despite bipartisan AI risk discussions. Close odds balance persistent concerns over deepfakes, cybersecurity threats, and ethical issues against GOP aversion to mandates. Tipping factors include a major AI incident, tech industry endorsements, or 2026 midterm gains by Democrats spurring compromise legislation.

question: Will Elon Musk be richest person on March 31?
description:

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Bloomberg Billionaires Index (https://www.bloomberg.com/billionaires/). If the data for the specified date is not released by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the Forbes Real-Time Billionaires List will be used (https://www.forbes.com/real-time-billionaires/#6aa3f0213d78). If neither source provides the specified date's data by April 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve according to the latest data point available on the Bloomberg Billionaires Index.

eventMetadata.context_description: Elon Musk's dominant 99.2% implied probability stems from his insurmountable net worth lead—currently over $320 billion per Forbes' real-time tracker—fueled by Tesla's recent stock rebound amid AI and robotaxi hype, plus SpaceX's soaring valuation from Starship milestones and government contracts. Trader consensus reflects minimal volatility risk, with no rival like Larry Ellison (Oracle-driven) or Jensen Huang (Nvidia surge) within striking distance before March 31. Upsets would require catastrophic Tesla plunges (e.g., regulatory hits) or unprecedented windfalls elsewhere, such as a massive Ellison acquisition or Buffett Berkshire blockbuster, but historical billionaire index trends show such swings as rare outliers in stable markets.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly backs Elon Musk at 88.5% implied probability to remain the world's richest person by December 31, 2026, fueled by his net worth surging past $420 billion on Tesla's post-election stock boom—up over 50% since November—and escalating SpaceX and xAI valuations amid AI hype. Musk's cultural dominance as a pop icon and his pivotal role advising President-elect Trump promise regulatory boosts for Tesla autonomy and Starship, cementing his lead. Challengers like Jensen Huang (2%), riding Nvidia's AI chip frenzy, and Larry Ellison (1.3%), buoyed by Oracle cloud deals and Tesla holdings, show momentum but lack Musk's breadth; legacy players such as Warren Buffett (1.2%) grapple with age and market shifts.

This market will resolve according to the name ranked #1 on the Bloomberg Billionaires Index on December 31, 2026, 5:30 PM ET.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Bloomberg Billionaires Index (https://www.bloomberg.com/billionaires/). If the data for the specified date is not released by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the Forbes Real-Time Billionaires List will be used (https://www.forbes.com/real-time-billionaires/#6aa3f0213d78). If neither source provides the specified date's data by January 2, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve according to the latest data point available on the Bloomberg Billionaires Index.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly backs Elon Musk at 88.5% implied probability to remain the world's richest person by December 31, 2026, fueled by his net worth surging past $420 billion on Tesla's post-election stock boom—up over 50% since November—and escalating SpaceX and xAI valuations amid AI hype. Musk's cultural dominance as a pop icon and his pivotal role advising President-elect Trump promise regulatory boosts for Tesla autonomy and Starship, cementing his lead. Challengers like Jensen Huang (2%), riding Nvidia's AI chip frenzy, and Larry Ellison (1.3%), buoyed by Oracle cloud deals and Tesla holdings, show momentum but lack Musk's breadth; legacy players such as Warren Buffett (1.2%) grapple with age and market shifts.

question: Will Frank Ocean release a new song in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed artist officially releases a new song between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM PT (Los Angeles time). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Officially released means that a newly released song or single is officially available for download or streaming (not including live performances, recordings of live performances, or leaks) by the resolution date.

Live versions, re-releases, deluxe editions, or similar non-original or altered versions of previously released songs will not count. Songs where the listed artist appears only as a secondary or featured performer, rather than the primary artist, will also not count. 

If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on the credits of the below-listed resolution sources (e.g., the song ‘girl, get up.’ counts as a new release for SZA as she is, equally to Doechii, labeled “Main Artist” in the Spotify credits to the song, regardless of feature indication in the title).

For listed groups of artists, tracks released by individual members, sub-units, or under any designation other than the listed group name will not count toward resolution.

The primary resolution sources will be official streaming and download platforms. In cases of ambiguity over how a song/album is labeled, the listed resolution sources will be consulted in the following order of precedence: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, and Deezer.

events.title: Which artists will release a new song in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed artist officially releases a new song between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM PT (Los Angeles time). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Officially released means that a newly released song or single is officially available for download or streaming (not including live performances or leaks) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, remixes, or alternate versions of previously released songs will not count. Songs where the listed artist appears only as a secondary or featured performer, rather than the primary artist, will also not count. 

If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them.

For listed groups of artists, tracks released by individual members, sub-units, or under any designation other than the listed group name will not count toward resolution.

The resolution source will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors artists like Taylor Swift and Beyoncé, with implied probabilities above 80% for new song releases in 2026, driven by their consistent output cycles—Swift's rapid post-Eras Tour momentum and Beyoncé's Cowboy Carter follow-up buzz from recent interviews hinting at ongoing projects. Lower odds trail for hiatus-takers like Adele (around 40%), amid her reluctance on new music post-2021's 30 album, and Rihanna, stalled by Fenty empire priorities despite 2022's tease. Key catalysts include potential 2025 award show announcements and holiday fan events, where stars often drop release hints; watch Swift's re-recordings wrap-up and Beyoncé's Renaissance tour extensions as bullish signals amid streaming era demands for annual drops. Unpredictability looms from contract disputes or personal breaks.

Re-releases, remixes, or alternate versions of previously released songs will not count. Songs where the listed artist appears only as a secondary or featured performer, rather than the primary artist, will also not count. 

If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them.

For listed groups of artists, tracks released by individual members, sub-units, or under any designation other than the listed group name will not count toward resolution.

The resolution source will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors artists like Taylor Swift and Beyoncé, with implied probabilities above 80% for new song releases in 2026, driven by their consistent output cycles—Swift's rapid post-Eras Tour momentum and Beyoncé's Cowboy Carter follow-up buzz from recent interviews hinting at ongoing projects. Lower odds trail for hiatus-takers like Adele (around 40%), amid her reluctance on new music post-2021's 30 album, and Rihanna, stalled by Fenty empire priorities despite 2022's tease. Key catalysts include potential 2025 award show announcements and holiday fan events, where stars often drop release hints; watch Swift's re-recordings wrap-up and Beyoncé's Renaissance tour extensions as bullish signals amid streaming era demands for annual drops. Unpredictability looms from contract disputes or personal breaks.

question: Will Olivia Rodrigo release a new song in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed artist officially releases a new song between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM PT (Los Angeles time). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Officially released means that a newly released song or single is officially available for download or streaming (not including live performances, recordings of live performances, or leaks) by the resolution date.

Live versions, re-releases, deluxe editions, or similar non-original or altered versions of previously released songs will not count. Songs where the listed artist appears only as a secondary or featured performer, rather than the primary artist, will also not count. 

If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on the credits of the below-listed resolution sources (e.g., the song ‘girl, get up.’ counts as a new release for SZA as she is, equally to Doechii, labeled “Main Artist” in the Spotify credits to the song, regardless of feature indication in the title).

For listed groups of artists, tracks released by individual members, sub-units, or under any designation other than the listed group name will not count toward resolution.

The primary resolution sources will be official streaming and download platforms. In cases of ambiguity over how a song/album is labeled, the listed resolution sources will be consulted in the following order of precedence: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, and Deezer.

question: Will the 10-year Treasury yield hit 4.5% before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Treasury 10-year yield reaches or is higher than the listed value for any date between November 11, 2025 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is the Department of the treasury, specially the data listed under "Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates" for the column "10 Yr" (see: https://home.treasury.gov/resource-center/data-chart-center/interest-rates/TextView?type=daily_treasury_yield_curve&field_tdr_date_value=2025).

events.title: How high will 10-year Treasury yield go before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Treasury 10-year yield reaches or is higher than the listed value for any date between November 11, 2025 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is the Department of the treasury, specially the data listed under "Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates" for the column "10 Yr" (see: https://home.treasury.gov/resource-center/data-chart-center/interest-rates/TextView?type=daily_treasury_yield_curve&field_tdr_date_value=2025).

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 70% implied probability to the 10-year Treasury yield peaking above 4% before 2027, a surge fueled by Friday's yield spike to 4.26%—highest since May—after a blockbuster 254,000 nonfarm payrolls report curbed expectations for deep Fed rate cuts. With yields closing near 4.15% amid resilient growth, sticky core PCE inflation at 2.7%, and ballooning fiscal deficits boosting supply pressures, market consensus tilts toward a higher peak versus September's Fed dot plot median of 3.4% terminal rate. Traders eye October CPI release on the 10th and November FOMC for shifts in cut pricing, though recession odds introduce downside uncertainty.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent robust US economic indicators, including September's 254,000 payroll gain exceeding forecasts, have propelled 10-year Treasury yields to 4.18%, curbing trader bets on sub-3% lows before 2027. Polymarket sentiment clusters around a 3.25-3.75% trough, aligning with CME FedWatch implying 100bps total cuts to a 3.75-4% fed funds terminal rate amid 2.4% core PCE inflation. Resilient growth and elevated term premiums provide a yield floor, though recession signals could trigger flight-to-safety dips. Traders eye October CPI (released softer at 2.4% headline), November 6-7 FOMC, and December jobs data as pivotal for resolution thresholds.

The resolution source for this market is the Department of the treasury, specially the data listed under "Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates" for the column "10 Yr" (see: https://home.treasury.gov/resource-center/data-chart-center/interest-rates/TextView?type=daily_treasury_yield_curve&field_tdr_date_value=2025).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent robust US economic indicators, including September's 254,000 payroll gain exceeding forecasts, have propelled 10-year Treasury yields to 4.18%, curbing trader bets on sub-3% lows before 2027. Polymarket sentiment clusters around a 3.25-3.75% trough, aligning with CME FedWatch implying 100bps total cuts to a 3.75-4% fed funds terminal rate amid 2.4% core PCE inflation. Resilient growth and elevated term premiums provide a yield floor, though recession signals could trigger flight-to-safety dips. Traders eye October CPI (released softer at 2.4% headline), November 6-7 FOMC, and December jobs data as pivotal for resolution thresholds.

question: Fed emergency rate cut before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) holds an emergency meeting after which the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is lowered between November 11, 2025 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An emergency meeting is defined as any unscheduled meeting called by the Federal Reserve Board or the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) apart from the regular eight pre-scheduled meetings for 2025 and the regular eight pre-scheduled meetings for 2026.

The resolution source will be official announcements from the Federal Reserve’s website (federalreserve.gov) or credible news sources reporting on the emergency meeting.

events.title: Fed emergency rate cut before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) holds an emergency meeting after which the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is lowered between November 11, 2025 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An emergency meeting is defined as any unscheduled meeting called by the Federal Reserve Board or the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) apart from the regular eight pre-scheduled meetings for 2025 and the regular eight pre-scheduled meetings for 2026.

The resolution source will be official announcements from the Federal Reserve’s website (federalreserve.gov) or credible news sources reporting on the emergency meeting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 83.5% implied probability of no Federal Reserve emergency rate cut before 2027, driven primarily by resilient U.S. economic data obviating crisis-level intervention. September's CPI inflation eased to 2.4% year-over-year, nearing the Fed's 2% target, while unemployment held steady at 4.1% amid solid nonfarm payroll gains, signaling a soft landing rather than recession. The Fed's recent 50-basis-point cut on September 18 marked orderly easing, with markets pricing in gradual 25-basis-point reductions at upcoming FOMC meetings, not panic moves. Absent shocks like banking turmoil or sharp GDP contraction, this backdrop supports sustained stability through 2026, though traders monitor October jobs data and holiday spending for pivot risks.

question: Will US GDP growth in 2026 be less than 0.5%?
description:
This market will resolve according to the seasonally adjusted real GDP growth rate for the United States in 2026, as reported in the Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) "Advance Estimate" release for Q4 of 2026, estimated to be released in January 2027.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.bea.gov/data/gdp/gross-domestic-product

Note: The relevant data will be the full-year real GDP growth rate as stated in the advance estimate, typically expressed as the percentage change from the annual level in 2025 to the annual level in 2026. Any revisions to this figure made after the release of the advance estimate will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders overwhelmingly favor U.S. GDP growth exceeding 2.5% in 2026 at 61.5% implied probability, driven by robust Q3 advance print of 2.8% annualized—surpassing consensus—and resilient labor market with unemployment steady at 4.1%. Fed rate cuts totaling 100bps since September signal a soft landing, bolstering productivity gains from AI investments and consumer spending. Fiscal tailwinds from potential post-election tax cuts further elevate optimism, contrasting Fed SEP median of 2.0% for 2026. Sub-1.5% buckets (<0.5% at 10.1%) reflect receding recession risks amid disinflation to 2.4% CPI, with November jobs data and December FOMC pivotal for revisions.

question: Will Spark launch a token by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Spark officially launches a governance token by 11:59 PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The token must be actively and publicly transferable and tradable. Announcements alone do not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Spark (https://x.com/spark), however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Will Hakeem Jeffries win the 2026 New York 8th District Democratic Primary?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Representative from New York’s 8th Congressional District.

If no 2026 New York 8th District Democratic House Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the New York Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: NY-08 Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Representative from New York’s 8th Congressional District.

If no 2026 New York 8th District Democratic House Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the New York Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hakeem Jeffries' commanding 92.5% implied probability in the NY-08 Democratic Primary stems from his entrenched incumbency as House Minority Leader, bolstered by dominant fundraising exceeding $2 million and strong past performances with 70%+ vote shares in prior cycles. Recent polling, such as Emerson College surveys showing him at 65% against fragmented challengers, reinforces trader consensus amid early voting underway ahead of the June 25 contest. Vance Bostic and Chi Ossé trail with minimal institutional support and lower name recognition, capturing slim odds. Realistic challenges would require a major scandal hitting Jeffries or unusually high progressive turnout boosting Ossé, though historical base rates for upset primaries against top leaders remain under 5%.

question: Will Half-Life 3 be announced before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Valve publicly and explicitly announces that Half-Life 3 is in production by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The announcement of the creation of any expansion or sequel not explicitly named "Half-Life 3" (e.g., Half-Life: Alyx, Half-Life 2: Episode One) will not be sufficient to qualify this market toward a "Yes" resolution. The game must have the words "Half-Life 3" in the title to qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Valve or a consensus of credible sources.

events.title: Will Half-Life 3 be announced before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Valve publicly and explicitly announces that Half-Life 3 is in production by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The announcement of the creation of any expansion or sequel not explicitly named "Half-Life 3" (e.g., Half-Life: Alyx, Half-Life 2: Episode One) will not be sufficient to qualify this market toward a "Yes" resolution. The game must have the words "Half-Life 3" in the title to qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Valve or a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent leak of assets from Valve's Deadlock playtest files—revealing a high-fidelity Gordon Freeman model, new crowbar, and other Half-Life 3 hallmarks—has propelled the Yes probability to 52.5%, reigniting trader optimism after years of silence since Half-Life: Alyx in 2020. This balances against Valve's storied secrecy and history of decade-long gaps between titles, with no official word despite massive fan demand and Steam's dominance fueling speculation. Key tippers include upcoming Steam Next Fest events, GDC 2025 panels, or surprise showcases like past Alyx reveals; a denial from Gabe Newell could crash odds, while teaser footage might surge them past 70%. Traders watch for concrete developer confirmation amid fluctuating leak credibility.

question: Israel and Syria normalize relations by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Israel and Syria officially announce the establishment of diplomatic relations by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Israel and the Syria, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Israel and Syria normalize relations by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Israel and Syria officially announce the establishment of diplomatic relations by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Israel and the Syria, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices low odds for Israel-Syria diplomatic normalization by the specified date, primarily due to Israel's heightened security concerns after Bashar al-Assad's ouster in December 2024 by Hay'at Tahrir al-Sham-led rebels. Recent Israeli airstrikes on Syrian military targets, including Damascus airport, signal persistent tensions, while the interim Syrian government's focus remains on internal stabilization rather than outreach. No official talks or confidence-building measures have emerged, echoing past stalls over Golan Heights sovereignty. Key watches include U.S. policy under incoming President Trump and Syria's leadership consolidation, which could shift probabilities amid regional volatility.

question: Israel and Syria normalize relations by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Israel and Syria officially announce the establishment of diplomatic relations by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Israel and the Syria, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Israel and Syria normalize relations by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Israel and Syria officially announce the establishment of diplomatic relations by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Israel and the Syria, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices low odds for Israel-Syria diplomatic normalization by the specified date, primarily due to Israel's heightened security concerns after Bashar al-Assad's ouster in December 2024 by Hay'at Tahrir al-Sham-led rebels. Recent Israeli airstrikes on Syrian military targets, including Damascus airport, signal persistent tensions, while the interim Syrian government's focus remains on internal stabilization rather than outreach. No official talks or confidence-building measures have emerged, echoing past stalls over Golan Heights sovereignty. Key watches include U.S. policy under incoming President Trump and Syria's leadership consolidation, which could shift probabilities amid regional volatility.

events.title: Israel and Indonesia normalize relations by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Israel and Indonesia officially announce the establishment of diplomatic relations by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Israel and Indonesia, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Israel-Indonesia diplomatic normalization leans heavily against near-term success, driven by Indonesia's unwavering support for Palestinian statehood amid the protracted Gaza conflict. As the world's largest Muslim-majority nation, Indonesia maintains no formal ties with Israel, a stance enshrined in its constitution requiring Middle East peace progress. Recent developments include indirect trade flows and President Prabowo Subianto's pragmatic overtures, such as past meetings with Israeli officials, but Gaza hostilities have stalled momentum from earlier Abraham Accords explorations. Key hurdles persist: Jakarta's UN votes backing Palestine and domestic political pressures. Watch for bilateral talks at upcoming forums like the G20 summit, though war escalation dims prospects.

question: GOP uses 'Nuclear Option' to break filibuster by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the US Senate passes a motion to invoke cloture and end debate on any part of the legislative process, with such a cloture motion receiving Yea votes from fewer than three-fifths of the Senators by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve "No".

Confirmations are considered part of the Senate’s “advice and consent” function, not part of the Senate’s “legislative process,” and will therefore not count towards this market's resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the U.S. government.

events.title: Will the GOP use 'Nuclear Option' to break filibuster by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the US Senate passes a motion to invoke cloture and end debate on any part of the legislative process, with such a cloture motion receiving Yea votes from fewer than three-fifths of the Senators by October 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve "No".

Confirmations are considered part of the Senate’s “advice and consent” function, not part of the Senate’s “legislative process,” and will therefore not count towards this market's resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the U.S. government.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket leans heavily against Republicans invoking the nuclear option to alter filibuster rules by the market's deadline, with Yes shares trading below 30%, reflecting GOP Senate divisions amid their 53-47 majority post-2024 elections. Key resistance stems from outgoing Leader Mitch McConnell's longstanding defense of the 60-vote cloture threshold and incoming Leader John Thune's caution, despite President-elect Trump's public calls to eliminate it for swift agenda passage via reconciliation. Recent catalysts include Trump's Truth Social posts urging reform and supportive rhetoric from senators like Josh Hawley, but no formal leadership commitment. Watch January 2025 Senate organization for rules votes that could shift odds.

question: Major U.S. bank bailout before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a U.S. bank with total assets exceeding $50 billion as of November 11, 2025 (see:https://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/lbr/current/), is bailed out by the U.S. federal government by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

A bailout is defined as any of these actions in direct response to directly related to solvency, liquidity, or capital adequacy concerns.

-Establishing a Federal Reserve emergency lending facility
-Creating an FDIC-assisted resolution or bridge bank
-A U.S. Treasury capital injection
-A publicly disclosed, regulatory-facilitated acquisition

An official announcement from the U.S. government that they are taking any of these actions will qualify regardless of if/when the action occurs.
 
Routine access to standing facilities (such as the discount window or BTFP) or participation in stress tests, capital raises, or ordinary supervision will not on their own qualify. 

If a bank experiences distress but is acquired privately without public intervention or coordination, this will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 76.5% implied probability for no major U.S. bank bailout before 2027, driven primarily by the robust results of the Federal Reserve's 2024 stress tests, where all 31 participating banks comfortably exceeded minimum capital requirements even under severe hypothetical recessions. Supporting this sentiment are strong Q3 earnings from major institutions like JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America, which beat expectations despite commercial real estate headwinds, bolstered by ample loan loss provisions and record-high CET1 ratios averaging over 12%. Post-2023 regional bank resolutions have fortified regulatory buffers via Basel III endgame rules, reducing systemic risks. While a sharp economic downturn remains a tail risk, current macroeconomic resilience and declining uninsured deposits underpin the market's confidence in banking stability through 2026.

question: Will India strike Pakistan by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if India initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Pakistani territory by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Indian military forces that impact Pakistani territory (e.g., if an Indian missile or drone hits a target within Pakistan’s borders, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones that are intercepted before reaching Pakistani territory, as well as surface-to-air missile strikes, will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether debris lands on Pakistani soil or causes damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or any operation conducted by Indian ground forces will not qualify as a strike under this market.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for an Indian strike on Pakistan in the near term, driven by heightened rhetoric following the June 9 Reasi terror attack in Jammu and Kashmir that killed nine civilians, which India attributes to Pakistan-based militants like Lashkar-e-Taiba. Prime Minister Modi's vows of a "strong response" and briefings to counterparts at the SCO summit have fueled speculation, yet official channels emphasize evidence-sharing and diplomacy amid nuclear deterrence and U.S. calls for restraint. No confirmed military mobilizations exist, with historical precedents like the 2019 Balakot strikes requiring major provocations. Upcoming SCO defense ministers' talks in Qingdao could ease tensions or escalate rhetoric, while monsoon conditions limit cross-border operations.

question: Will Hamas agree to disarm by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Hamas officially announces it will disarm in the Gaza Strip by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only credible announcements, for example by the widely acknowledged leadership of Hamas, will qualify.

For the purposes of this market, "disarm" refers to a public commitment to relinquish or dismantle its military, whether partially or completely, in the Gaza Strip.

Announcements of partial disarmament (e.g., surrendering a class of weapons or agreeing to disarm in stages or a certain region) will qualify as long as it is part of an acknowledged disarmament process.

Only official announcements will qualify. Informal statements, plans contingent on future conditions, statements of intent without a formal policy directive, or any other statements that do not constitute a formal policy announcement will not be considered.

Primary resolution sources will include official statements from Hamas leadership; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting confirming a policy of disarmament has been instituted will also qualify.

events.title: Will Hamas agree to disarm by...?

Announcements of partial disarmament (e.g., surrendering a class of weapons or agreeing to disarm in stages or a certain region) will qualify as long as it is part of an acknowledged disarmament process.

Only official announcements will qualify. Informal statements, plans contingent on future conditions, statements of intent without a formal policy directive, or any other statements that do not constitute a formal policy announcement will not be considered.

Primary resolution sources will include official statements from Hamas leadership; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting confirming a policy of disarmament has been instituted will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Stalled ceasefire talks between Israel and Hamas drive low trader consensus on Hamas agreeing to disarm by the deadline, as Hamas officials have repeatedly rejected demilitarization demands in recent Qatar- and Egypt-mediated rounds. The latest Doha negotiations in late October collapsed over core disputes, including Israeli troop withdrawal from Gaza, permanent truce terms, and Hamas retaining armed capabilities for "defense." Hamas statements affirm no surrender of weapons, while Israel insists on dismantling militant infrastructure. Upcoming indirect talks, potentially influenced by U.S. diplomatic pressure post-election, represent key catalysts, though historical impasses suggest limited progress absent major concessions.

question: Ukraine election held by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if national elections for the parliament and/or presidency of Ukraine are held between February 12, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is based on whether national elections are actually held in Ukraine within the specified dates. Merely scheduling an election will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes".

If elections are officially scheduled for a date outside this market's timeframe, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Ukraine, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Ukraine election held by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if national elections for the parliament and/or presidency of Ukraine are held between February 12 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is based on whether national elections are actually held in Ukraine within the specified dates. Merely scheduling an election will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes".

If elections are officially scheduled for a date outside of 2025, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Ukraine, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing Russian invasion and repeated parliamentary extensions of martial law remain the dominant barriers to Ukraine's presidential election, constitutionally prohibited under wartime conditions since February 2022. President Zelenskyy's term formally ended in May 2024, but parliament approved the latest 90-day extension on August 15, pushing it to mid-November amid active combat. Recent polls indicate strong public support (over 70%) for postponement until security improves, while opposition figures and some Western allies debate legitimacy but accept the delay. Traders price low near-term probabilities, reflecting consensus that elections hinge on martial law lift or ceasefire—next extension vote expected early November, with no ceasefire imminent.

question: Will Russia capture Kostyantynivka by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the Kostyantynivka railroad station located on Pravoberezhna vulytsia by March 31, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET.

The railroad station will be considered captured if any part of the train station icon is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the railroad station, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Train Station Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka3.jpg

Train Station Location in Kostyantynivka: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka2.jpg

Kostyantynivka Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka1.jpg

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/hZ6ch5jVRvkNFY317

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

events.title: Will Russia capture Kostyantynivka by...?
events.description: This is a market on the likelihood of Russia capturing Kostyantynivka by a specific date.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces' incremental advances in Donetsk Oblast, capturing nearby villages like Shevchenko and progressing toward Pokrovsk, represent the primary driver of trader sentiment on Kostyantynivka's potential capture, a key Ukrainian logistics hub with rail connections supporting frontline defenses. Recent reports indicate Russian troops are within 5-10 km of the town's outskirts amid intensified assaults, but Ukrainian reinforcements and minefields have slowed momentum, with no confirmed breaches as of late November 2024. Harsh winter weather and Ukrainian drone strikes add uncertainty, while ongoing mobilization efforts on both sides could shift dynamics. Traders weigh these factors against historical advance rates, pricing in low near-term resolution probabilities; watch for DeepState map updates or official claims of control.

question: China coup attempt before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a widely reported coup attempt in China at any point between November 11, 2025 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

A coup attempt is defined as a coordinated and deliberate effort by military, security forces, or other state actors (or factions thereof) to overthrow or unlawfully seize control of the Chinese government or its leadership. Revolutionary actions by non-state forces, isolated protests, or general unrest will not alone qualify.

Claims by the Chinese government that a coup attempt was foiled—such as through arrests or disruption of a planned operation—will only count toward a "Yes" resolution if credible independent sources widely report and explicitly characterize the event as a coup attempt. Foiled plots or arrests that occur without any attempted execution of the coup will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: China coup attempt before 2027?
events.description:

Revolutionary actions by non-state forces, isolated protests, or general unrest will not alone qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors no coup attempt in China before 2027 at 94.5%, driven by Xi Jinping's unchallenged consolidation of power within the Chinese Communist Party and People's Liberation Army. Recent purges of senior military officials, including Rocket Force commanders in late 2024, underscore anti-corruption efforts to bolster loyalty rather than signal instability. No verified reports from official state media or primary sources indicate factional threats, elite defections, or unrest, while China's extensive surveillance and internal security apparatus deter organized challenges. Ephemeral social media rumors of plots have consistently dissipated without evidence, aligning with historical stability since Xi's 2012 rise and low base rates for successful coups in one-party states ahead of the 2027 Party Congress.

question: Will Russia rejoin the G7 before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Russia officially rejoins the Group of Seven (G7) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the title of the group changes (e.g., to "G8"), this market will still resolve to "Yes" so long as that group remains functionally similar to the G7.

A formal rejoining must be acknowledged by a consensus of G7 member states to qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from G7 member states, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will Russia rejoin the G7 before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Russia officially rejoins the Group of Seven (G7) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the title of the group changes (e.g., to "G8"), this market will still resolve to "Yes" so long as that group remains functionally similar to the G7.

A formal rejoining must be acknowledged by a consensus of G7 member states to qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from G7 member states, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russia's suspension from the G8 in 2014 following its annexation of Crimea remains the foundational barrier to rejoining the G7, with the Ukraine invasion since 2022 widening the geopolitical divide through escalating Western sanctions and isolation. Recent G7 summits, including the June 2024 gathering in Italy, issued strong communiques condemning Moscow's actions, pledging $50 billion in loans backed by frozen Russian assets, and extending economic restrictions—signaling unified opposition without any hint of reinstatement discussions. Ongoing hostilities and NATO's bolstered eastern flank further entrench this stance, justifying traders' 89.9% consensus on "No" as reflecting entrenched alliance priorities over reconciliation before 2027.

question: Modi out by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Narendra Modi ceases to be Prime Minister of India for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Narendra Modi's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Narendra Modi and the government of India; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Narendra Modi and the government of India; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: NDA coalition's 293-seat Lok Sabha majority, secured in June 2024 elections, anchors trader confidence at 88.6% against Narendra Modi exiting office by end-2026, with full-term polls not due until 2029. Recent Union Budget concessions to allies TDP and JD(U)—including infrastructure funds for Andhra Pradesh and Bihar—eased early coalition frictions, while Modi's inauguration of major projects like the Chenab rail bridge reinforces governance continuity. No credible challenges, such as no-confidence motions or resignations, have emerged amid steady GDP growth above 6%, though opposition critiques on unemployment persist without derailing stability. State assembly votes in Maharashtra and Haryana loom as tests but pose limited national risk.

question: Will Trump send Tomahawk to Ukraine by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Trump administration or the U.S. government announces that it is sending, has sent, or will send Tomahawk cruise missiles to Ukraine by March 31, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

A qualifying statement within the specified timeframe will suffice regardless of when the missiles are sent.

For the purposes of this market, official policy announcements from the Trump administration or the U.S. government will qualify. Informal statements, leaks, or speculative remarks will not be sufficient.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the Trump administration, the U.S. government, or the government of Ukraine. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used if no direct official statement is available.

events.title: Will Trump send Tomahawk to Ukraine by...?

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Trump administration or the U.S. government announces that it is sending, has sent, or will send Tomahawk cruise missiles to Ukraine by October 31, 2025, at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

A qualifying statement within the specified timeframe will suffice regardless of when the missiles are sent.

For the purposes of this market, official policy announcements from the Trump administration or the U.S. government will qualify. Informal statements, leaks, or speculative remarks will not be sufficient.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the Trump administration, the U.S. government, or the government of Ukraine. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used if no direct official statement is available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for President-elect Trump authorizing Tomahawk missile shipments to Ukraine by the specified date, driven by his repeated pledges to swiftly negotiate an end to the Russia-Ukraine war rather than escalate arms transfers. Trump's campaign criticized Biden-era aid packages exceeding $60 billion, favoring direct talks with Putin and Zelenskyy over additional weaponry like advanced cruise missiles, which the U.S. has withheld to date. Recent transition developments, including no pro-aid signals from nominees like Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, reinforce de-escalation expectations. Key upcoming events include Trump's January 20 inauguration and potential early summits with Ukraine, which could clarify policy but underscore negotiation priorities over munitions.

question: Will Israel launch a major ground offensive in Lebanon by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a major ground offensive in Lebanon by March 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "major ground offensive" is defined as a large-scale military operation that involves more than 1,000 Israeli ground forces entering Lebanese territory that was not under Israeli control at the start of the offensive. This excludes smaller raids, special operations, or limited incursions directly bordering buffer zones already under Israeli control.
 
The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.
